channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How Trump shifted from banning TikTok to saving it
item.description: During his first term as president, Donald Trump led the effort to ban TikTok, the hugely popular video-sharing site he said posed a threat to U.S. national security. But on the eve of his return to the White House, the president-elect is being hailed as the app’s savior.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy set to depart DOGE
item.description: Biotech entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy confirmed on Monday he will not serve on President Trump’s “Department of Government Efficiency” (DOGE) commission, leaving Tesla CEO Elon Musk to singularly lead the advisory group.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Everything leaving Hulu in February 2025
item.description: Although we rate Hulu as one of the best streaming services, two of our favorite movies on the platform are leaving in a few weeks. Here's everything leaving Hulu in February 2025.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Tech leaders stand together at Trump inauguration in Capitol Rotunda
item.description: Some of the country's leading technology leaders are together at the Capitol Rotunda for President-elect Trump's inauguration as the industry gets closer to the incoming leader's orbit.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: If the iPhone SE 4 has this feature, I won’t be able to recommend the iPhone 16 anymore
item.description: The rumored iPhone SE 4 may bring Apple Intelligence compatibility to Apple’s cheapest handset, which would seemingly be a genuinely pro-consumer move from Cupertino.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Fresh Nvidia RTX 5090 and 5080 stock rumors again suggest that next-gen GPUs will not be easy to buy
item.description: Latest rumors on RTX 5000 stock have me even more worried that it’ll be a frustrating scramble to buy these GPUs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft warns its January Windows updates may fail if this Citrix software is installed
item.description: Certain Citrix software is blocking Microsoft’s January 2025 Patch Tuesday update – here’s how to fix it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cloud infrastructure spending more than doubles in the third quarter of 2024
item.description: Spending on compute and storage infrastructure for cloud deployments has surged to unprecedented heights, with 115.3% year-over-year increase in the third quarter of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Spoiler alert! Just about every single Samsung Galaxy S25 spec shows up in the latest leak
item.description: We're not far off the official launch of the Galaxy S25 series, but the leaks are continuing – including these spec lists.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: After 10 years of Apple Watch, the company reveals the one thing it got wrong about its users
item.description: Apple has revealed it expected sporty Apple Watch users to embrace exercise when they launched Apple Watch, while less sporty users would focus on other things. Turns out they were wrong.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Chinese electric hypercar jumps over spikes, potholes while driving itself
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the $236,000 Yangwang U9 electric hypercar boasts 1,287 horsepower and can jump obstacles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intersec 2025: Middle East CISOs focus on AI
item.description: The growing threat posed by Agentic AI and disinformation has become one of the most critical challenges in the cybersecurity landscape.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Retorno rápido de la inversión frente a innovación: los objetivos contrapuestos de los CIO
item.description: La estrategia de los CIO en materia de inteligencia artificial (IA) es hoy una historia de agendas contrapuestas: mejoras rápidas de la productividad, por un lado, e innovaciones a largo plazo que cambian las reglas del juego, por otro.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Instagram announces new video app amid TikTok troubles
item.description: Instagram announced a new video creation app Sunday amid legal struggles faced in the U.S. by TikTok, another social media platform with a focus on visual content.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Melania meme coin launches following Trump's new cryptocurrency
item.description: The website for the coin describes “Melania Memes” as “fungible crypto assets created and tracked on the Solana blockchain.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Emanuel blasts Trump for 'flexibility' on TikTok position
item.description: Rahm Emanuel, President Biden’s ambassador to Japan, sharply criticized President-elect Trump for reversing his position on TikTok, saying the app still poses a danger to the American people so long as it remains under the control of a China-based company.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Tech titans bend the knee to Trump
item.description: President-elect Trump’s inauguration will bring together some of the country’s most prominent technology leaders this week, underscoring the results of a months-long push by the industry to reconcile with the incoming president.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Supreme Court’s decision on TikTok may have just turned a generation off politics
item.description: Opinions can be shaped very quickly, and grudges last a long time. Donald Trump knows that better than most. Joe Biden ought to, but seemingly doesn’t.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Army readies new AI guidance based on lessons learned
item.description: The Army is looking at commercial AI use cases that can serve large populations of users, ideally without running up huge bills The post Army readies new AI guidance based on lessons learned first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 3 big predictions for AI in financial services in 2025
item.description: As we look ahead to 2025, the financial services landscape will be shaped not by AI as a standalone innovation, but by how it enables smarter, faster, and more personalized experiences. Here are three key predictions for how AI will transform the industry: 1.
item.dc:creator: Ben Borodach, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok refugees or propagandists? China’s RedNote users share mixed responses to U.S. influx
item.description: Users of the Chinese social media app RedNote welcomed “TikTok refugees” from the United States with selfies and messages on Wednesday, as Beijing said it encouraged stronger cultural ties with other countries in response to the sudden influx.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: How to test out the new Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: Nintendo unveiled its highly anticipated Nintendo Switch 2 that’s set to be released later this year. Nintendo didn’t provide a launch date, but said it would share more at an April 2 event.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation
